We claim none of these mechanisms as novel - fatigue-aware learning-to-defer (FALCON), cost-sensitive deferral under workload constraints (DeCCaF), trajectory-level guarding, and reviewer-fatigue/flooding attacks are all prior art we cite. Our contribution is an open-source agent-oversight system that operationalizes and measures them in the LLM-agent action-gating setting, turning "is my guard good?" from a guess into a curve. The inverted-U and the flooding attack are modeling results that motivate a human study.

AI.tldr: 本文指出LLM智能体安全审核的"人类回环"模式中，核心难点并非审批门控本身，而是"哪些行为需要停止"的判断。通过125个对抗性样本实验发现：审核员对"风险"判定一致性低（Fleiss' kappa=0.52）；将门控建模为不对称成本下的选择性分类可量化其操作极限；当审核员疲劳被内生化时，系统安全性随升级率呈倒U型曲线，过多人类监督反而降低安全性。贡献在于开源了可度量上述机制的智能体行为门控系统。
AI.motivation: 现有LLM智能体安全评估基于两个错误假设：存在客观的"风险"标签，以及人类审核员是完美且无限可用的预言机。作者旨在揭示这些假设的缺陷，并建立可度量的安全门控评估框架。
AI.method: 构建125个对抗性加权的智能体行为数据集，进行多审核员标注一致性分析；将门控问题形式化为不对称成本下的选择性分类；建模审核员疲劳作为内生变量，分析升级率对安全性的影响；提出负载感知策略抵御洪水攻击。
AI.result: 审核员间一致性中等（Fleiss' kappa=0.52）；硬输入上门控无法安全自动决策；当审核员疲劳被建模时，安全性随升级率呈倒U型，完全升级并非最优；负载感知策略可抵御通过使审核员疲劳来夹带恶意行为的洪水攻击。
AI.conclusion: 智能体监督不仅是分类问题，更是资源分配问题——人类注意力有限，门控的升级策略是消费它的方式。贡献在于开源系统将"门控是否良好"从猜测转变为可量化的曲线，倒U型和洪水攻击结果为后续人类实验提供动机。

title: Evaluating the Impact of Task Granularity on Catastrophic Forgetting in Continual Learning
summary:
Catastrophic forgetting, the abrupt loss of previously acquired knowledge upon learning new information, remains the central challenge in Continual Learning. This project investigates whether the order in which a model learns information affects how well it retains knowledge. Specifically, we ask: does learning general categories first (like "animals" vs "vehicles") before learning specific classes (like "dog" vs "cat") reduce forgetting compared to learning all classes at once?
  We test three approaches on CIFAR-100: (1) Coarse-to-Fine: train on 2 super-classes, then expand to 10 specific sub-classes, (2) Fine-to-Coarse: train on 10 sub-classes, then group into 2 super-classes, and (3) Flat: train on all 10 classes from the start. We use Elastic Weight Consolidation (EWC) to prevent forgetting during transitions. Our hypothesis is that learning general patterns first creates a stable foundation that helps the model retain knowledge when learning more detailed distinctions. We evaluate using standard metrics (accuracy, precision, recall, F1) plus continual learning metrics like backward transfer and forgetting rates. This work could inform how we design learning sequences for real-world systems that need to learn incrementally.

AI.tldr: 本文研究了连续学习中类别学习顺序对灾难性遗忘的影响，通过CIFAR-100数据集比较了"从粗到细"、"从细到粗"和"平坦"三种学习策略，并采用弹性权重巩固（EWC）防止遗忘，实验假设先学习通用类别再学习具体类别能建立稳定基础从而减少遗忘。
AI.motivation: 探究类别学习顺序（先学通用类别再学具体类别）是否比一次性学习所有类别更能减少灾难性遗忘，从而为现实系统中增量学习的设计提供指导。
AI.method: 在CIFAR-100上设计三种学习策略：(1) Coarse-to-Fine：先训练2个超类，再扩展至10个具体子类；(2) Fine-to-Coarse：先训练10个子类，再合并为2个超类；(3) Flat：从头开始训练所有10个类别。使用EWC（弹性权重巩固）防止过渡阶段的遗忘，评估指标包括准确率、精确率、召回率、F1以及反向迁移和遗忘率等连续学习指标。
AI.result: 未在摘要中给出具体实验结果，仅说明将使用标准指标和连续学习指标进行评估，并假设Coarse-to-Fine策略能减少遗忘。
AI.conclusion: 先学习通用类别再学习具体类别的学习顺序可能有助于建立稳定的知识基础，从而减少灾难性遗忘，对增量学习系统的设计有指导意义。

title: AlloSpatial: Agentic Harness Framework for Spatial Reasoning in Foundation Models
summary: Multimodal Foundation Models (MFMs) have made substantial progress, yet remain fragile in spatial reasoning over the physical world. A key bottleneck lies in their inability to transform local egocentric observations into a global allocentric spatial representation. To address this, we propose AlloSpatial, an agentic framework for allocentric spatial cognition in foundation models. AlloSpatial introduces World2Mind, a plug-and-play cognitive mapping sandbox that converts egocentric observations into structured allocentric priors, including Allocentric-Spatial Trees and route maps that support querying object topology, geometric relations, passability, and trajectories. To utilize these priors reliably under noisy reconstruction and ambiguous visual evidence, AlloSpatial introduces a Spatial Reasoning Harness for tool-use judgment, modality-decoupled cue collection, and geometry-semantic arbitration. We further internalize this process in Qwen3-VL through cold-start reinforcement learning with a harness-gated trajectory-level reward. Experiments on VSI-Bench and MindCube show that AlloSpatial improves proprietary models by 5%-18% in a training-free setting, while ASTs alone support strong spatial reasoning even when visual inputs are removed. The trained AlloSpatial agents further outperform larger general-purpose models and competitive spatial baselines, suggesting that structured allocentric representations, active tool use, and verifiable reasoning offer a promising route toward spatially capable foundation models.

AI.tldr: 本文提出AlloSpatial框架，通过World2Mind认知映射沙箱将自我中心观测转化为以物体为中心的空间先验（Allocentric-Spatial Trees和路线图），并设计空间推理控制框架进行工具使用判断与几何-语义仲裁，结合冷启动强化学习训练，在空间推理任务上显著提升基础模型性能。
AI.motivation: 多模态基础模型在物理世界空间推理中表现脆弱，核心瓶颈在于无法将局部自我中心观测转化为全局以物体为中心的空间表征。
AI.method: 提出AlloSpatial框架，包含：(1) World2Mind认知映射沙箱，将自我中心观测转化为结构化以物体为中心的先验（Allocentric-Spatial Trees和路线图）；(2) Spatial Reasoning Harness，用于工具使用判断、模态解耦线索收集和几何-语义仲裁；(3) 基于控制门控轨迹级奖励的冷启动强化学习（在Qwen3-VL上实现）。
AI.result: 在VSI-Bench和MindCube上，AlloSpatial在免训练设置下使专有模型提升5%-18%；仅使用ASTs即使移除视觉输入也能支持强空间推理；训练的AlloSpatial智能体超越更大规模通用模型和竞争性空间基线。
AI.conclusion: 结构化的以物体为中心表征、主动工具使用和可验证推理为构建具备空间能力的基础模型提供了有前景的路径。

title: Semantic Quorum Assurance: Collective Certification for Non-Deterministic AI Infrastructure
summary: As large language model (LLM) agents are integrated into autonomous cloud operations, distributed systems face a semantic reliability problem: proposer agents can generate production mutations, such as modifying IAM policies, opening firewall security groups, or executing data exports, that are syntactically valid and statically authorized but operationally unsafe. Classical distributed consensus protocols replicate deterministic state transitions but do not evaluate the safety of the proposed intent. To address this gap, we introduce Semantic Quorum Assurance (SQA), a control-plane primitive for governing non-deterministic agentic infrastructure. SQA represents proposals as declarative execution contracts bound to cryptographic evidence chains and routes them to a diverse panel of read-only, sandboxed validator agents. SQA aggregates their judgments under a risk-adaptive quorum predicate that enforces model and archetype diversity, adjusts weights based on calibrated assurance scores, and respects archetype-specific vetoes. Admitted proposals execute only through a sovereign execution gate. We instantiate SQA in a cloud-native control plane and formalize a correlated cognitive failure model for non-deterministic validators. On 500 infrastructure-inspired mutation scenarios, with safety results reported on held-out safe/unsafe trials excluding ambiguous scenarios, SQA reduces unsafe approval from 18.5% for single-agent validation to 0.3% while adding median validation latency of 1.45--4.12 seconds across the studied risk buckets.

AI.tldr: 针对LLM智能体在云操作中可能生成语法有效但操作不安全的突变问题，提出语义法定人数保障（SQA）机制。SQA将变更提议表述为声明性执行合约，交由多样化只读验证智能体小组评估，通过风险自适应法定人数谓词聚合判断，并经过主权执行门控后执行。实验表明，SQA将不安全批准率从18.5%降至0.3%，验证延迟为1.45-4.12秒。
AI.motivation: LLM智能体在自动化云操作中会产生语义可靠性问题：语法有效且静态授权但操作不安全的变更（如修改IAM策略、开放防火墙端口），而经典分布式共识协议只复制确定性的状态转换，不评估提议意图的安全性。
AI.method: 提出语义法定人数保障（SQA）控制平面原语：将提议表示为附加密码学证据链的声明性执行合约，路由给由多样化只读沙箱验证智能体组成的小组；采用风险自适应法定人数谓词聚合判断，该谓词强制模型和原型多样性、基于校准的保障分数调整权重、尊重原型特定否决权；通过主权执行门控控制提议执行。
AI.result: 在500个基础设施变更场景上，SQA将不安全批准率从单智能体验证的18.5%降至0.3%，各风险桶的中位验证延迟为1.45-4.12秒。
AI.conclusion: SQA有效解决了LLM智能体在云操作中的语义可靠性问题，显著降低不安全操作批准率，验证延迟在可接受范围内。

title: An Effective Router for Vision-Language Model Selection
summary: Vision-language models (VLMs) with varying performance and resource requirements are widely deployed, making it difficult for users to select the most appropriate one among numerous VLM candidates. Existing work reveals the performance paradox phenomenon in language models and focuses on routing methods to solve it. However, developing a router for VLM selection is still a critical yet challenging problem, which primarily faces: 1) lack of specialized data, 2) ineffective feature representation, and 3) rigid model space and costly adaptation. In this paper, we construct a multimodal dataset for VLM selection, containing the outputs of seven mainstream VLMs on 32,626 unique image-text queries. We then propose ARMS, a router for VLM selection. ARMS enhances input signals with VLM profiles, employs a simple but effective architecture to improve representations of queries and VLM capabilities. To improve ARMS' adaptation to new VLMs, we propose two extension training strategies: incremental training and independent training. Experimental results on both in-distribution and out-of-distribution test sets demonstrate the effectiveness of ARMS. In particular, using our training strategy, ARMs (only 800M in size) can adapt to a broader VLM space and defeat commercial models like GPT-4o that are hundreds of times larger in scale. Our code, models, and datasets are available in the anonymous repository.

AI.tldr: 本文提出ARMS路由系统用于视觉-语言模型（VLM）选择，通过构建包含7个主流VLM在32,626个图像-文本查询上的多模态数据集，利用VLM配置文件增强输入信号，并采用简单有效的架构改进查询和VLM能力表示。同时提出增量训练和独立训练两种扩展策略以适应新VLM。实验表明，仅800M参数的ARMS可击败规模大数百倍的商业模型如GPT-4o。
AI.motivation: 现有VLM性能与资源需求各异，用户难以选择最合适的模型。尽管已有研究关注语言模型的性能悖论现象并采用路由方法解决，但在VLM选择上仍面临三大挑战：缺乏专门数据集、特征表示无效、模型空间僵化且适应成本高。
AI.method: 构建包含7个主流VLM在32,626个独特图像-文本查询上的多模态VLM选择数据集；提出ARMS路由系统，通过VLM配置文件增强输入信号，采用简单但有效的架构改进查询和VLM能力的表示；提出增量训练和独立训练两种扩展策略以支持新VLM的适应。
AI.result: 在分布内和分布外测试集上均验证了ARMS的有效性。仅800M参数的ARMS能够适应更广泛的VLM空间，并击败规模大数百倍的商业模型GPT-4o。
AI.conclusion: ARMS有效解决了VLM选择问题，通过构建专用数据集、优化特征表示和灵活的扩展策略，实现了轻量级模型对大模型的性能超越，为VLM选型提供了实用解决方案。

title: CausShield: Sample Reconstruction-Resilient Vertical FL via Causal Representation Learning
summary: Vertical federated learning (VFL) is a distributed learning paradigm that leverages vertically partitioned features across isolated parties without sharing raw samples; however, it remains vulnerable to active sample reconstruction attacks. Existing defenses fail to achieve a satisfactory trade-off between model utility and privacy protection, due to either suppressing task-relevant information alongside privacy-sensitive features or relying on end-to-end supervised training to converge the defense module, which exposes the model to early-epoch vulnerability. To address this challenge, we adopt a structural causal model (SCM) insight and construct CausShield. From a task-learning standpoint, causal features within a raw sample are those that are directly relevant and contributory to the learning objective, whereas non-causal features are task-irrelevant but often encode sample-specific private information, thereby facilitating reconstruction. Importantly, we lay a theoretical foundation to prove this insight. CausShield thus decomposes the shared representations between the client and the coordinating server in VFL into task-relevant and task-irrelevant components to ensure full-cycle privacy protection. Nonetheless, the decomposition is inherently challenging due to the dual objectives of preserving model utility while mitigating privacy leakage. We address this via a carefully formulated optimization problem, which is solved through unsupervised representation learning. We further theoretically prove that CausShield preserves the convergence behavior of standard VFL. Extensive experiments compare CausShield against seven SOTAs, including InvL (USENIX Security'25), and evaluate robustness against advanced reconstruction attacks such as URVFL (NDSS'25). Results demonstrate that CausShield consistently outperforms in privacy protection, model utility, and computational efficiency.

AI.tldr: 针对纵向联邦学习中的主动样本重构攻击，本文提出基于结构因果模型的CausShield框架，通过将共享表示分解为任务相关和任务无关组件，在保护模型效用的同时实现全周期隐私防护，理论证明其保持收敛性，实验优于七种SOTA方法（含USENIX Security'25和NDSS'25的攻击方法）。
AI.motivation: VFL面临主动样本重构攻击威胁，现有防御方法要么在抑制隐私特征时连带抑制任务相关信息，要么依赖端到端监督训练导致早期轮次易受攻击，无法实现模型效用与隐私保护的良好权衡。
AI.method: 基于SCM洞察，将原始样本中的因果特征（任务相关）与非因果特征（任务无关但编码隐私信息）区分，将VFL中客户端与服务器的共享表示分解为任务相关和任务无关两部分；通过精心设计的无监督表示学习优化问题实现分解，并理论证明了该方法保持标准VFL的收敛行为。
AI.result: 与七种SOTA方法（含USENIX Security'25的InvL和NDSS'25的URVFL攻击）相比，CausShield在隐私保护、模型效用和计算效率三个维度上均一致优于对比方法。
AI.conclusion: CausShield通过因果驱动的表示分解策略，有效解决了VFL中效用-隐私权衡难题，实现了全周期的隐私防护且不影响收敛性，为VFL安全提供了一种理论扎实且实践高效的解决方案。

title: Diverse Thinking Schemata Elicit Better Reasoning in Large Language Models
summary: Large reasoning models (LRMs) have attracted increasing attention for their ability to solve complex mathematical problems by generating extended reasoning chains. In this work, we focus on two critical yet underexplored aspects of the reasoning process: reasoning transitions capturing the distinct transitions between reasoning steps and answer candidates reflecting the variety of solution paths produced by the model. We collectively define these two aspects as thinking schemata. We observe a correlation between the diversity of thinking schemata and model performance, which motivates us to enhance diversity as a means to further improve reasoning potential. To this end, we propose Diverse Schemata Policy Optimization (DiScO), a framework that first endows the model with schemata awareness, then encourages diversity through reinforcement learning, and further promotes diverse reasoning at inference time. Experiments on multiple mathematical reasoning benchmarks demonstrate that DiScO consistently outperforms standard group relative policy optimization. Beyond accuracy, human-annotated analyses show that DiScO substantially improves the model's ability to recover from erroneous initial attempts. Overall, our work suggests the important role that diversity of the thinking schemata plays and points to scaling along the diversity dimension as a promising research direction.

AI.tldr: 本文提出多样式策略优化（DiScO）框架，通过增强推理过程中的思维图式（包括推理转换和答案候选）多样性，提升大型推理模型在数学推理任务中的表现，实验表明该方法优于标准策略优化，并能有效提升模型从错误初始尝试中恢复的能力。
AI.motivation: 大型推理模型虽能通过生成扩展推理链解决复杂数学问题，但其推理过程中的两个关键方面——推理转换（推理步骤间的不同转换）和答案候选（模型产生的多样化解路径）——尚未被充分探索。作者观察到思维图式多样性与模型性能之间存在相关性，因此希望通过增强多样性来进一步释放模型的推理潜力。
AI.method: 提出多样式策略优化（DiScO）框架，包含三个步骤：首先赋予模型图式感知能力（schemata awareness），使模型能够识别和区分不同的思维图式；然后通过强化学习（基于组相对策略优化）鼓励模型生成多样化的思维图式；最后在推理阶段进一步促进多样化的推理路径生成。
AI.result: 在多个数学推理基准上，DiScO一致优于标准组相对策略优化（GRPO）。此外，人工标注分析表明，DiScO显著提升了模型从错误初始尝试中恢复的能力。
AI.conclusion: 思维图式的多样性在推理过程中扮演重要角色，沿多样性维度进行规模化扩展是一个有前景的研究方向。

title: Noise-Adaptive High-Probability Regret Bounds for Online Convex Optimization
comment: Accepted to 2026 European Conference on Machine Learning and Principles and Practice of Knowledge Discovery in Databases(ECML-PKDD 2026)
summary: We study high-probability regret bounds for online convex optimization (OCO) with strongly convex losses and establish three results that resolve open questions at the intersection of noise adaptivity, feedback structure, and constraint satisfaction. For the full-information setting with sub-Gaussian stochastic gradients, we prove a noise-adaptive high-probability regret bound in which the martingale deviation term scales with the noise level $σ$ rather than the gradient bound $G$, yielding a multiplicative improvement of $G/σ$ over the classical Azuma-Hoeffding baseline. Our analysis introduces an exponential supermartingale argument that bypasses the bounded-difference requirement of Freedman's inequality, enabling direct treatment of unbounded sub-Gaussian noise without truncation artifacts. For bandit feedback, we prove a minimax lower bound: the high-probability regret scales linearly in $\log(1/δ)$, in contrast to the $\sqrt{\log(1/δ)}$ confidence cost under full information. This constitutes a formal separation in the confidence cost of strongly convex OCO across feedback models. Regarding constrained OCO with stochastic constraints satisfying a Slater condition, we provide simultaneous high-probability guarantees for both cumulative regret and long-run constraint violation, achieving $\mathcal{O}(\sqrt{T\log(m/δ)})$ regret and $\mathcal{O}(\sqrt{T}/(ζδ) + m\sqrt{T\log(m/δ)})$ violation. Synthetic experiments corroborate all theoretical predictions.

AI.tldr: 本文研究了强凸损失下在线凸优化（OCO）的高概率遗憾界，在噪声自适应、反馈结构和约束满足三个方向取得了突破。全信息设定下，证明噪声自适应的遗憾界，将Azuma-Hoeffding基线中的梯度界$G$改进为噪声水平$σ$，实现$G/σ$倍的乘性提升；bandit反馈下，证明高概率遗憾与$\log(1/δ)$线性相关，与全信息下的$\sqrt{\log(1/δ)}$形成正式分离；随机约束下，给出累积遗憾和长期约束违反的同时高概率保证。合成实验验证了所有理论预测。
AI.motivation: 解决强凸OCO中噪声自适应、反馈结构与约束满足三个方向上的开放问题，弥补高概率遗憾界中尚未被充分探索的理论空白。
AI.method: 全信息设定：引入指数超鞅论证，绕过Freedman不等式所需的有界差分条件，直接处理无界次高斯噪声；bandit反馈：推导极小极大下界；约束OCO：利用Slater条件给出同时高概率保证。
AI.result: 全信息：噪声自适应界，偏差项从$G$降为$σ$；Bandit：遗憾与$\log(1/δ)$线性相关，与全信息形成正式分离；约束OCO：遗憾$\mathcal{O}(\sqrt{T\log(m/δ)})$，违反$\mathcal{O}(\sqrt{T}/(ζδ) + m\sqrt{T\log(m/δ)})$。
AI.conclusion: 全信息下噪声自适应的高概率遗憾界是最优的；bandit反馈的高概率遗憾成本显著高于全信息；Slater条件下可同时实现遗憾和约束违反的高概率保证。

title: RTL-BenchLS: A Large-Scale Benchmark for RTL Reasoning and Generation with Large Language Models
summary: LLM-based RTL generation and reasoning is a promising direction for hardware design automation. High-quality benchmarks are critical infrastructure for tracking progress in this direction. However, existing RTL benchmarks face inherent limitations in both scale and task scope. The designs they cover are typically small and simple, and the tasks focus almost entirely on specification-to-RTL generation. Frontier models' performance already saturates on the existing benchmarks. Scaling these benchmarks up is fundamentally difficult because aligned labels are required for benchmarking, such as specifications and testbenches. Such aligned high-quality data are rarely available for real-world designs. We introduce RTL-BenchLS, a large-scale benchmark addressing both limitations above. It contains over 10,000 formally verified Verilog designs, covering substantially larger and more complex designs than existing benchmarks. Beyond specification-to-RTL generation, we propose three novel tasks that jointly evaluate reasoning and generation: round-trip reasoning, masked-content reasoning, and repository-issue reasoning. The first two are self-supervised, which directly resolves the scaling bottleneck. All tasks are verified through formal equivalence checking without any manual testbenches. We evaluate eight LLMs on RTL-BenchLS. Even the best model reaches only 23% on natural-language round-trip reasoning, 28% on masked-content reasoning, and 12% on repository-issue fixing. RTL-BenchLS is substantially more challenging than existing benchmarks. It leaves ample room for future improvement and offers guidance for developing LLM-based methods for hardware design.

AI.tldr: RTL-BenchLS是一个包含超10,000个形式验证Verilog设计的大规模基准测试集，涵盖更大更复杂的设计，并提出三种新任务（往返推理、掩码内容推理、仓库问题推理），以解决现有RTL基准在规模和任务范围上的局限性。评估8个LLM，最佳模型仅达到23%/28%/12%，表明该基准极具挑战性。
AI.motivation: 现有RTL基准存在规模和任务范围的根本性限制：设计规模小且简单，任务几乎仅限于规格到RTL生成，前沿模型性能已饱和；且由于需要对齐标签（如规格和测试平台），扩展基准规模极其困难。
AI.method: 构建包含10,000+形式验证的Verilog设计的大规模基准，提出三类自监督新任务（往返推理、掩码内容推理、仓库问题推理），通过形式等价性检查自动验证，无需人工测试平台。
AI.result: 在8个LLM上评估，最佳模型在自然语言往返推理上仅23%，掩码内容推理28%，仓库问题修复12%，远低于现有基准的饱和性能。
AI.conclusion: RTL-BenchLS显著比现有基准更具挑战性，为LLM在硬件设计自动化领域的发展留下了充分提升空间，并为开发基于LLM的硬件设计方法提供指导。

title: SafeECGMatch: Calibration-Aware Joint Frequency and Time Space Semi-Supervised Learning for Open-Set ECG Classification
comment: 8 pages. Accepted to the KDD-UC 2026 (ACM International Conference on Data Mining and Knowledge Discovery - Undergraduate Consortium 2026)
summary: Electrocardiogram (ECG) classification models often suffer from severe label scarcity, making semi-supervised learning (SSL) an attractive strategy for reducing annotation costs. In clinical settings, however, unlabeled pools frequently contain out-of-distribution (OOD) anomalies or diagnostic groups absent from the labeled set. Standard SSL forces incorrect pseudo-labels onto these unseen classes, producing overconfident predictions. To address this, we propose SafeECGMatch, a calibration-aware safe SSL framework for single-label ECG classification under label distribution mismatch. Methodologically, SafeECGMatch employs a dual-branch architecture extracting time-frequency latent representations via ECG-specific augmentations. Crucially, it dynamically aligns confidence with empirical accuracy through adaptive label smoothing and temperature scaling, calibrating both the multiclass classifier and the OOD detector across temporal and spectral domains. This joint optimization allows trustworthy OOD rejection and reliable pseudo-labeling. Evaluated on the PTB-XL and PhysioNet/CinC Challenge benchmarks, SafeECGMatch achieves state-of-the-art accuracy and calibration, advancing reliable knowledge discovery in physiological time-series. Code is available at https://github.com/labhai/SafeECGMatch.

AI.tldr: SafeECGMatch提出了一种校准感知的安全半监督学习框架，用于解决心电图分类中标签分布不匹配（未标注集中存在OOD异常类别）的问题，通过在双分支架构中结合自适应标签平滑和温度缩放实现置信度校准，在PTB-XL等基准上取得SOTA效果。
AI.motivation: ECG分类模型面临严重的标签稀缺问题，半监督学习虽可降低标注成本，但临床未标注数据中常包含未见过的OOD异常类别，标准SSL会强制给这些类别分配错误的伪标签，产生过度自信的预测。
AI.method: 采用双分支架构提取时频域潜在表示，通过ECG特定数据增强；利用自适应标签平滑和温度缩放动态对齐置信度与经验准确率，联合优化多类分类器和OOD检测器，实现跨时域和频域的校准。
AI.result: 在PTB-XL和PhysioNet/CinC Challenge基准上，SafeECGMatch在准确率和校准性能上均达到SOTA水平。
AI.conclusion: SafeECGMatch通过校准感知的安全半监督学习，有效处理了标签分布不匹配场景下的ECG分类问题，推动了生理时间序列中的可靠知识发现。

title: Baichuan-M4: A Clinical-Grade Medical Agent System for Continuous Care
summary: Baichuan-M4 is Baichuan Intelligence's clinical-grade medical large model, designed for \emph{continuous care} rather than single-turn medical question answering. It is built as a coordinated medical agent system around three pillars: \textbf{Baichuan-Harness}, a unified runtime that keeps reinforcement-learning training and real-world deployment consistent while enforcing action constraints, tool use, long-term patient memory, and multi-agent coordination; a \textbf{core reasoning model} trained with a continuous-care reinforcement-learning framework that integrates span-level reward modeling (SPAR++), reasoning-path compression, curriculum learning, and stabilized policy optimization; and a \textbf{clinical tool layer} for patient-memory management, authoritative evidence-based retrieval, and multimodal medical perception across documents, X-rays, and dermatology. On a cross-dimensional medical evaluation suite, Baichuan-M4 attains leading results in static medical knowledge and safety, dynamic OSCE-style consultation, long-context clinical memory, evidence-based retrieval, medical document OCR, and multimodal image understanding, while lowering the hallucination rate to 3.3\%.

AI.tldr: Baichuan-M4 是百川智能开发的临床级医疗大模型，通过统一运行时环境、连续护理强化学习框架和临床工具层三大支柱构建协调医疗代理系统，在多维度医学评估中取得领先结果，幻觉率降至3.3%。
AI.motivation: 现有医疗大模型多局限于单轮问答，缺乏对患者持续护理的能力。作者旨在构建一个能够支持长期、连续医疗护理场景的临床级医疗代理系统，覆盖记忆管理、工具使用、多模态感知和强化学习训练等多方面需求。
AI.method: 模型围绕三大支柱构建：1) Baichuan-Harness统一运行时，保持强化学习训练与部署一致，实施动作约束、工具使用、长期患者记忆和多智能体协调；2) 核心推理模型，采用连续护理强化学习框架，集成跨度级奖励建模(SPAR++)、推理路径压缩、课程学习和稳定策略优化；3) 临床工具层，负责患者记忆管理、权威循证检索以及文档、X光片、皮肤科图像等多模态医学感知。
AI.result: 在跨维度医学评估套件中，在静态医学知识与安全性、动态OSCE式问诊、长上下文临床记忆、循证检索、医学文档OCR及多模态图像理解等多个维度均取得领先结果，幻觉率降至3.3%。
AI.conclusion: Baichuan-M4通过统一的医疗代理系统设计和连续护理强化学习框架，有效提升了医疗大模型在复杂临床场景中的表现，显著降低了幻觉率，为临床级医疗大模型的发展提供了新范式。

title: When Behavioral Safety Evaluation Fails: A Representation-Level Perspective
summary: Large Language Model (LLM) safety has often been evaluated at the behavior level, which provides limited evidence of internal robustness, as these evaluations target outputs rather than representation-level vulnerability under intervention. We formalize this discrepancy as the audit gap: the difference between behavioral safety and robustness under intervention. To study this gap, we construct dissociated models that preserve safe outward behavior while remaining vulnerable in the latent space. We introduce an intervention-based evaluation framework to test model robustness through soft interventions in parameter and latent spaces, including harmful fine-tuning and layer-wise latent perturbations. To formalize the evaluation, we propose the Latent Vulnerability Score (LVS) to measure how easily harmful behavior can be elicited by bounded latent perturbations. Using this evaluation framework, we show that behavioral safety metrics are insufficient measures of representation-level robustness across multiple safely and unsafely aligned state-of-the-art models. Notably, dissociated models show substantially elevated LVSs despite comparable refusal behavior under harmful intervention, with intermediate representations being the most sensitive to intervention. Our results suggest that behavioral safety evaluation alone provides an incomplete picture of model robustness, motivating representation-aware audits of latent vulnerability and observable behavior.

AI.tldr: 本文提出“审计差距”概念，即LLM行为安全与表征层鲁棒性之间的不一致。通过构建“解离模型”（外在安全但潜在空间脆弱），并引入基于干预的评估框架和潜在脆弱性评分（LVS），证明仅靠行为安全评估不足以反映模型的表征级鲁棒性。
AI.motivation: 现有LLM安全评估主要关注行为层面（输出），缺乏对表征层干预下内部鲁棒性的评估。作者希望揭示行为安全与表征鲁棒性之间的差距。
AI.method: 1) 构建“解离模型”（dissociated models），保持安全外在行为但潜在空间存在脆弱性；2) 提出干预评估框架，包括参数空间（有害微调）和潜在空间（逐层潜在扰动）的软干预；3) 设计潜在脆弱性评分（LVS）量化在有限潜在扰动下诱导有害行为的难易程度。
AI.result: 解离模型在有害干预下虽保持拒绝行为，但LVS显著升高；中间表征层对干预最为敏感；多个安全/非安全对齐模型均表现出行为安全与表征鲁棒性的不一致。
AI.conclusion: 仅靠行为安全评估无法完整反映模型鲁棒性，需要结合表征级脆弱性审计，推动表征感知的安全评估范式。

title: The Token Not Taken: Sampling, State, and the Variability of AI Agent Outputs
summary: Agentic AI systems can behave differently across runs: the same request may produce a different plan, a different tool call, a different code edit, or a different final answer. Such variability arises from several layers that are often conflated. A foundation model is a large pretrained model, usually adaptable to many downstream tasks, that maps an input context to predictions over outputs. In many current agents, that model is embedded in an orchestration loop that plans, calls tools, observes results, and updates state. One explicit intrinsic source of variability in such systems is token generation: the model computes scores over possible next tokens, the scores are converted into probabilities, and a decoder may sample tokens using a pseudo-random number generator. A small sampled token difference can then propagate upward into a different tool call, code path, search query, or agent state. Other sources of variability are extrinsic to token sampling, including changing environments, live data, serving infrastructure, batch effects, and numerical details. By separating these layers, the manuscript clarifies what it means to call agentic AI systems stochastic, when such variability can be reproduced under matched conditions, and why deterministic execution need not imply identical behavior in deployed settings.

AI.tldr: 本文系统分析了Agentic AI系统中行为变异的来源，区分了token生成的内在随机性（如采样解码）与外部因素（如环境变化、实时数据、基础设施等），阐明了随机性、可复现性与确定性执行在部署场景下的区别。
AI.motivation: Agentic AI系统在不同运行中可能产生不同的计划、工具调用、代码编辑或最终答案，但这种变异的来源常常被混淆。作者希望通过分离不同层次的变异性来源，澄清何时可以复现、何时不能，以及确定性执行为何不意味着行为完全相同。
AI.method: 本文采用概念分析方法，将Agentic AI系统的变异性来源分为内在（token生成层面的概率采样）和外在（环境、实时数据、服务基础设施、批处理效应、数值细节等）两类，并对这些层次进行解耦讨论。
AI.result: 区分了token生成层面的内在随机性（通过伪随机数生成器采样解码）与外在变异性来源；指出微小的采样差异可能向上传播导致不同的工具调用、代码路径、搜索查询或智能体状态；明确了确定性执行并不等同于部署场景下的行为一致性。
AI.conclusion: 通过分离这些层次，本文为理解Agentic AI系统的随机性提供了清晰框架，解释了何种条件下变异性可以复现，以及为何确定性执行在部署中不一定产生相同行为。

title: Beyond Homophily: Towards Generalized Graph Reconstruction Attack and Defense
summary: Graph neural networks (GNNs) are widely deployed on relational data, yet they can leak sensitive or proprietary information about the training graph adjacency, e.g., social ties, transactions, and interactions. This work studies graph reconstruction attacks (GRA), a form of model inversion that reconstructs the training adjacency from a trained GNN, given different levels of attacker-side information. We first provide a systematic characterization of when and why adjacency becomes recoverable through features, labels, embeddings, and predictions, with leakage modulated by graph homophily, heterophily, and the model's inductive bias. Motivated by these findings, we view GNN inference through a Markov chain approximation lens, treating the layered forward computation as a chain of topology-dependent representations. Building on this view, we develop complementary attack and defense methods. On the attack side, we propose MC-GRA (+), which reconstructs the adjacency by optimizing a surrogate adjacency whose GNN-induced representations align with those of the target model at each layer. On the defense side, we propose MC-GPB (+), which suppresses adjacency-dependent information throughout the representation chain while aiming to preserve classification accuracy under a privacy-utility trade-off. Experiments across homophilic/heterophilic graph benchmarks and GNNs show that our attacks improve reconstruction fidelity over prior methods, while our defenses reduce reconstruction success with only minor accuracy loss.

AI.tldr: 本文系统研究了图神经网络（GNN）的图重构攻击（GRA）问题，从攻击与防御两个角度提出方法：攻击端通过马尔可夫链近似逐层对齐表示来重构训练邻接矩阵（MC-GRA+），防御端通过抑制表示链中的邻接依赖信息来平衡隐私与效用（MC-GPB+）。实验表明攻击提升重构保真度，防御在轻微精度损失下有效降低重构成功率。
AI.motivation: GNN在关系数据上广泛应用，但可能泄露训练图邻接的敏感信息（如社交关系、交易记录）。现有研究对邻接为何以及何时可被重构缺乏系统性理解，且缺乏有效的攻击与防御方法。
AI.method: 提出马尔可夫链近似视角，将GNN的前向计算视为拓扑依赖表示的链式过程。攻击方法MC-GRA(+)通过优化替代邻接使各层表示与目标模型对齐来重构邻接；防御方法MC-GPB(+)在表示链中抑制邻接相关信息，同时保持分类精度，实现隐私-效用权衡。
AI.result: 在同质/异质图基准和多种GNN上，攻击方法相比先前方法提升了重构保真度；防御方法在仅轻微精度损失下显著降低了重构成功率。
AI.conclusion: 邻接重构的可行性受图同质性/异质性及模型归纳偏置影响。马尔可夫链近似视角为理解GNN信息泄漏提供了统一框架，所提攻防方法在该框架下有效。

title: LATTEArena: An Evaluation Framework for LLM-powered Tabular Feature Engineering (Extended Version)
summary: Feature engineering remains essential for tabular data analysis, and Large Language Models (LLMs) have emerged as a promising paradigm for automating this process, giving rise to LLM-powered AuTomated Tabular feature Engineering (LATTE). However, the absence of standardized platforms prevents fair, cost-aware comparisons. Furthermore, complex methodological designs obscure the specific contributions of individual components; for example, although LFG integrates Tree-of-Thought, few-shot demonstrations, Monte Carlo Tree Search, and natural language generation, the isolated impact of each technique's competitive edge remains unquantified. To address these challenges, we introduce LATTEArena, the first competitive evaluation framework featuring: (1) a six-dimensional taxonomy decomposing 15 representative methods into reusable components; (2) a standardized modular arena for controlled comparison; (3) multi-dimensional assessments covering performance, cost, and robustness; and (4) component-level ablation quantifying each technique's competitive edge. Through extensive evaluations, we reveal 16 key findings, including: (1) Tree-of-Thought with Monte Carlo Tree Search achieves optimal cost-effectiveness; (2) RPN and Code output formats dominate classification and regression tasks, respectively. We publicly release the modular framework and over 4000 execution logs, enabling researchers to seamlessly pit new techniques against existing ones and advance LATTE.

AI.tldr: 本文提出LATTEArena，首个用于LLM驱动的表格特征工程（LATTE）的标准化评估框架，包含六维分类体系、模块化竞技场、多维度评估和组件级消融分析，基于15种方法和4000+执行日志揭示了16项关键发现。
AI.motivation: 当前LATTE领域缺乏标准化平台导致无法进行公平、成本感知的比较，且复杂的方法设计掩盖了各组件的独立贡献（如LFG中Tree-of-Thought、MCTS、少样本演示等各自的作用未被量化）。
AI.method: 提出了LATTEArena框架，包含：(1) 六维分类体系，将15种代表性方法分解为可复用的组件；(2) 标准化模块化竞技场用于受控比较；(3) 涵盖性能、成本和鲁棒性的多维度评估；(4) 组件级消融实验以量化每种技术的竞争优劣势。
AI.result: 通过广泛评估揭示了16项关键发现，例如：Tree-of-Thought结合蒙特卡洛树搜索实现了最优成本效益；RPN格式在分类任务中占优，Code输出格式在回归任务中占优。
AI.conclusion: 公开了模块化框架和4000+执行日志，使研究人员能够便捷地将新技术与现有方法进行比较，推动LATTE领域发展。

title: DICE: Entropy-Regularized Equilibrium Selection for Stable Multi-Agent LLM Coordination
summary: Multi-agent large language model (LLM) systems often fail to reliably outperform a single strong model equipped with best-of-N sampling. We argue that a core source of this instability is ill-posed equilibrium selection: current systems specify what information agents share, but not which coordination convention should be selected. We formalize a broad class of such systems as discounted incomplete-information Markov games and show that two common pathologies, oscillation between competing conventions and drift across them, can both induce unstable learning and linear Bayesian regret. To obtain a well-posed target, we introduce the Heterogeneous Quantal Response Equilibrium (HQRE), an entropy-regularized equilibrium concept with agent- and state-dependent temperatures. Under a monotonicity condition, HQRE is unique, admits linearly convergent mirror updates, and yields bounded Bayesian regret; the same condition yields rollout-measurable stability diagnostics. We instantiate this objective in two algorithms: DICE-PC, which coordinates frozen models through prompt-control actions, and DICE-FT, which performs parameter-efficient mirror fine-tuning. Across eleven benchmarks in four domains, DICE improves accuracy-cost trade-offs over strong within-class baselines; on reasoning and planning tasks, DICE-PC improves by 4.3 percentage points on average and DICE-FT by 8.5 points.

AI.tldr: 本文指出多智能体大语言模型系统常因不恰当的均衡选择（如震荡和漂移）导致性能不如单个强模型结合Best-of-N采样。作者将这类系统形式化为折扣不完全信息马尔可夫博弈，并提出异质分位数响应均衡（HQRE）作为唯一且稳定的学习目标。基于此设计了DICE-PC（提示控制）和DICE-FT（参数高效微调）两种算法，在11个基准测试中分别提升平均4.3和8.5个百分点。
AI.motivation: 多智能体LLM系统常不稳定，无法可靠超越单个强模型加Best-of-N采样，核心原因在于缺乏明确定义的协调惯例（均衡选择问题），导致学习过程出现震荡和漂移。
AI.method: 将多智能体系统形式化为折扣不完全信息马尔可夫博弈；提出异质分位数响应均衡（HQRE），引入熵正则化及智能体/状态依赖的温度参数；在单调性条件下证明HQRE唯一性、镜像更新线性收敛和有界贝叶斯遗憾；基于HQRE设计DICE-PC（提示控制协调冻结模型）和DICE-FT（参数高效镜像微调）两种算法。
AI.result: 在四个领域11个基准上，DICE在准确率-成本权衡上超越同类强基线；DICE-PC平均提升4.3个百分点，DICE-FT平均提升8.5个百分点。
AI.conclusion: 通过引入HQRE这一良定义的均衡概念，可以有效解决多智能体LLM系统中的协调不稳定问题，实现可证明收敛和可测量的稳定性诊断，并在实际任务中取得显著性能提升。

title: A Multi-Agent System for IPMSM Design Optimization via an FEA-AI Hybrid Approach
summary: Interior permanent magnet synchronous motor (IPMSM) design requires balancing conflicting objectives and multi-physics constraints, while modern optimization workflows face three bottlenecks: manual problem setup, high finite element analysis (FEA) cost, and unreliable surrogate-based search in sparse or out-of-distribution regions. To address these limitations, we propose an end-to-end automated IPMSM design optimization framework that integrates retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) for structured problem definition with an uncertainty-aware FEA-AI hybrid optimization pipeline. A Design agent, connected to a motor textbook through RAG, provides domain-knowledge-based options and engineering tips, and compiles an optimization card and a design-of-experiments plan for AI-model training. A Training agent automates electromagnetic FEA, records geometry-validation and solver-failure logs, analyzes failed geometries using ANOVA-based data analysis and LLM reasoning, and invokes a Design Sampling agent to redefine the design space and generate additional samples. An Optimization agent performs GA-based search with uncertainty-driven switching: low-uncertainty candidates are evaluated by AI-surrogate inference, whereas high-uncertainty and reliability-critical Pareto-front or top-K candidates are corrected by high-fidelity FEA and reused for iterative retraining. The framework converts manual, experience-dependent configuration into a reproducible workflow that balances computational cost and prediction reliability. Experimental results under a matched high-fidelity FEA budget show that the proposed hybrid approach achieves better objective performance while maintaining low and further reducible predictive uncertainty, outperforming FEA-only search, which is limited by early budget exhaustion, and AI-only search, which converges to a low-confidence optimum.

AI.tldr: 提出了一种端到端自动化的内置式永磁同步电机(IPMSM)设计优化框架，结合检索增强生成(RAG)进行结构化问题定义，以及不确定性感知的有限元分析(FEA)-人工智能(AI)混合优化流水线，通过设计智能体、训练智能体和优化智能体的协同工作，在同等高保真FEA预算下实现了优于纯FEA搜索和纯AI搜索的客观性能与预测可靠性平衡。
AI.motivation: IPMSM设计需要在相互冲突的目标和多物理场约束之间寻求平衡，而现代优化工作流面临三个瓶颈：手动问题设置繁琐、有限元分析计算成本高昂、基于代理的搜索在稀疏或分布外区域不可靠。
AI.method: 提出端到端自动化框架，包含三个核心组件：(1) 通过RAG连接电机教科书的Design agent，提供领域知识并生成优化卡片和实验设计计划；(2) Training agent自动化电磁FEA，记录几何验证和求解器失败日志，利用ANOVA和LLM推理分析失败几何，并调用Design Sampling agent重新定义设计空间和生成额外样本；(3) Optimization agent执行基于遗传算法的搜索，采用不确定性驱动的切换机制：低不确定性候选由AI代理推理评估，高不确定性和可靠性关键的帕累托前沿或top-K候选则由高保真FEA校正并用于迭代重训练。
AI.result: 在同等高保真FEA预算下，该混合方法实现了更好的客观性能，同时保持了低且可进一步降低的预测不确定性，优于受早期预算耗尽限制的纯FEA搜索和收敛到低置信度最优解的纯AI搜索。
AI.conclusion: 该框架将手动、依赖经验的配置转化为可复现的工作流，有效平衡了计算成本与预测可靠性。

title: ConSteer-RL: Steering Reasoning Capabilities in Large Language Models via Confidence-Aware Reinforcement Learning
summary: Reinforcement Learning from Verifiable Rewards (RLVR) has recently become a key paradigm for improving the reasoning abilities of Large Language Models (LLMs), yet it remains limited by sparse binary rewards and its ignorance of model-internal uncertainty. In this paper, we propose ConSteer-RL, a simple yet effective framework that integrates token-level confidence signals derived from model log-probabilities into RLVR training. Specifically, building upon the Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO) framework, we construct a confidence-aware reward by aggregating per-token probabilities into a scalar confidence score and incorporating it into an awareness-based reward shaping mechanism that penalizes overconfident errors while reinforcing correct and confident reasoning. Experimental results demonstrate that ConSteer-RL consistently outperforms strong GRPO baselines, achieving average improvements of 2.3%-4.0% across different model scales.

AI.tldr: 本文提出 ConSteer-RL 框架，通过在 GRPO 中引入基于模型 log-probabilities 的 token 级置信度信号，构建置信感知奖励以惩罚过度自信的错误并强化正确且自信的推理，实验表明该方法在多个模型规模上平均提升 2.3%-4.0%。
AI.motivation: 现有 RLVR 方法受限于稀疏二元奖励且忽略模型内部不确定性，导致无法有效区分正确/错误推理中的置信度差异。
AI.method: 基于 GRPO 框架，聚合逐 token 概率为标量置信度分数，设计置信感知奖励整形机制：惩罚过度自信错误，强化正确且自信的推理。
AI.result: 在多个模型规模上，ConSteer-RL 持续优于强 GRPO 基线，平均提升 2.3%-4.0%。
AI.conclusion: 将模型内部不确定性信号（置信度）纳入 RLVR 训练是一种简单有效的改进思路，可显著提升 LLM 推理能力。

title: Personalization Meets Safety:Mechanisms,Risks,and Mitigations in Personalized LLMs
summary: Large Language Models (LLMs) have enabled increasingly personalized interactions by adapting to users' preferences, contexts, and long-term histories. However, the mechanisms that enable personalization also expand the safety landscape in ways not systematically addressed by existing literature. Existing reviews typically focus either on personalization or safety, leaving their intersection largely unexplored. We present the first comprehensive, safety-aware review of personalized LLMs. We organize personalization along three dimensions-user representation, personalization paradigm, and evaluation-and introduce a unified taxonomy of safety risks. At the representation level, we analyze risks arising from diverse user representations. Across mainstream personalization paradigms, we delineate vulnerabilities inherent to prompting, retrieval augmentation, parameter fine-tuning, reinforcement learning, Mixture-of-Experts (MoE), pruning, agent frameworks, and multimodal personalization, and synthesize mitigation strategies across the model lifecycle. Beyond these fine-grained risks, we characterize paradigm-agnostic safety risks arising from personalized adaptation. We further summarize personalized datasets and evaluation methodologies. Through a case study of OpenClaw, we analyze deployment trends in personalized agent ecosystems. Our analysis reveals three structural inadequacies in existing research: safety is evaluated as user-invariant rather than relational, personalization techniques are analyzed in isolation rather than in composition, and evaluation frameworks cannot capture emergent long-term risks. By jointly examining personalized representations, personalization paradigms, safety risks, defenses, and evaluation methods, we provide a unified framework for developing safe personalized LLMs and highlight key directions for future research.

AI.tldr: 本文首次对个性化大语言模型（LLMs）的安全性进行了全面综述，从用户表征、个性化范式和评估三个维度组织个性化，并提出统一的安全风险分类体系。通过分析OpenClaw案例，揭示了现有研究在安全性评估、技术孤立分析和长期风险捕获三方面的结构性缺陷。
AI.motivation: 现有文献往往分别聚焦于个性化或安全性，缺乏对二者交叉域的系统性研究。个性化机制扩展了安全风险面，但未被充分关注，亟需一个安全性感知的个性化LLMs综述来统一认识并指导未来研究。
AI.method: 沿三个维度（用户表征、个性化范式、评估）组织个性化，引入统一的安全风险分类体系；在表征层分析不同用户表征的风险，在范式层梳理提示、检索增强、微调、强化学习、MoE、剪枝、智能体框架及多模态个性化中的脆弱性，并综合模型全生命周期的缓解策略；通过OpenClaw案例研究分析部署趋势。
AI.result: 识别出三种结构性不足：安全性评估是用户不变而非关系性的；个性化技术被孤立分析而非组合分析；评估框架无法捕获新兴长期风险。通过案例研究揭示了个性化智能体生态系统的部署趋势。
AI.conclusion: 提出了开发安全个性化LLMs的统一框架，强调了未来研究的关键方向，包括将安全性视为关系性属性、组合分析个性化技术、以及设计能捕获长期风险的评估方法。

title: Constraint-Aware Optimization for Robust Protein Stability Prediction
summary: Multimodal $ΔΔG$ predictors integrating protein language models with inverse-folding representations achieve strong in-distribution accuracy on the Megascale dataset but exhibit limited robustness on out-of-distribution (OOD) proteins, persistent forward-reverse bias on paired-mutation benchmarks, and under-representation of rare stabilizing mutations. Existing approaches address these limitations primarily through additional architectural components, leaving optimization-level intervention comparatively underexplored. We introduce a constraint-aware optimization framework combining Balanced Mean Squared Error, a Siamese anti-symmetric regularizer, and a novel OOD-margin consistency loss on the per-position feature representation, requiring no architectural changes to the SPURS backbone. Across eleven benchmarks and three random seeds, the framework improves Spearman correlation on S669 from 0.486 to 0.540 ($σ=0.002$ across seeds), matching the published SPURS baseline (0.50) without architectural modification, and on S461 from 0.653 to 0.711, with consistent smaller gains on five additional OOD datasets. A controlled diagnostic on Ssym reveals that anti-symmetric training does not eliminate systematic forward-reverse bias, indicating that gains arise through implicit regularization rather than exact thermodynamic constraint enforcement.

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种无需修改架构的约束感知优化框架（结合Balanced MSE、Siamese反对称正则化器和OOD-margin一致性损失），在SPURS骨架上将S669的Spearman相关系数从0.486提升至0.540，S461从0.653提升至0.711，但反对称训练并未消除前向-反向偏差，表明收益源于隐式正则化而非严格热力学约束。
AI.motivation: 现有多模态ΔΔG预测器在分布外蛋白质上鲁棒性有限，存在前向-反向偏差且对稀有稳定突变表征不足；现有方法主要通过额外架构组件解决，而优化层面的干预尚未被充分探索。
AI.method: 提出约束感知优化框架，包含三个组件：Balanced Mean Squared Error（平衡均方误差）、Siamese anti-symmetric regularizer（孪生反对称正则化器）和基于每个位置特征表示的OOD-margin一致性损失（新颖）。无需对SPURS骨干网络进行任何架构修改。
AI.result: 在11个基准和3个随机种子下，S669的Spearman相关性从0.486提升至0.540（σ=0.002），匹配已发表的SPURS基线（0.50）；S461从0.653提升至0.711；在5个额外OOD数据集上取得一致的小幅提升。但Ssym诊断实验表明反对称训练未能消除系统性前向-反向偏差。
AI.conclusion: 性能提升并非来自严格的热力学约束强制执行（反对称训练未消除偏差），而是源于隐式正则化效应；优化层面的干预无需架构修改即可有效提升模型鲁棒性。

title: Agent Economics: An Entropy-Controlled Pluralistic Alignment Framework for Preventing Artificial Hivemind in Autonomous Agents
summary: This study proposes the Behavioral Protocol Framework (BPF), an entropy-controlled pluralistic alignment framework designed to address two critical challenges in autonomous agent economies: the hivemind effect arising from excessive strategic convergence among agents and the lack of transparency in autonomous decision-making processes. The proposed BPF consists of three core modules: Mentalizing-based Social Intelligence (MbSI) grounded in Theory of Mind (ToM), Pluralistic Alignment (PA), and a Verifiable Execution Kernel (VEK). These modules are organically integrated within a closed-loop architecture that governs the entire lifecycle of agent behavior, from decision-making and execution to verification and feedback. To evaluate the proposed framework, a simulation environment implemented in Python and a Streamlit-based user interface will be developed. Through empirical experimentation, the study aims to examine whether the entropy-control mechanism of the PA module can effectively preserve strategic diversity among agents and mitigate collective convergence, while the VEK module provides a comprehensive and transparent audit trail of the decision-making process. The anticipated results are expected to demonstrate that the proposed framework can simultaneously enhance the stability, efficiency, and trustworthiness of autonomous agent economies. Consequently, this research offers a practical approach for developing robust, transparent, and accountable agent-native economic systems.

AI.tldr: 本文提出了行为协议框架（BPF），一个基于熵控制的多元对齐框架，通过心智化社会智能、多元对齐和可验证执行内核三个核心模块，解决自主智能体经济中的蜂巢思维效应和决策不透明问题。
AI.motivation: 自主智能体经济面临两大关键挑战：智能体之间过度战略趋同导致的蜂巢思维效应，以及自主决策过程缺乏透明度。现有方法未能同时解决这两个问题。
AI.method: BPF框架包含三个核心模块：基于心智理论的心智化社会智能（MbSI）、多元对齐（PA）模块（通过熵控制机制保持策略多样性）、可验证执行内核（VEK）模块（提供决策过程的可审计追踪）。三者集成在闭环架构中，覆盖智能体行为从决策、执行到验证和反馈的完整生命周期。
AI.result: 预期通过Python仿真环境和Streamlit界面进行实证实验，验证PA模块的熵控制机制能否有效保持策略多样性并缓解集体趋同，以及VEK模块能否提供全面的决策过程审计追踪。
AI.conclusion: 该框架有望同时增强自主智能体经济的稳定性、效率和可信度，为开发健壮、透明且可问责的智能体原生经济系统提供实用方案。

title: A Unifying View of Attention Sinks: Two Algorithms, Two Solutions
summary: When attention concentrates on a single token, a sink, what is the model actually computing? Attention sinks are ubiquitous in softmax transformers, yet this shared visual signature can hide fundamentally different algorithms. We show that visually similar sink patterns can reflect two distinct mechanisms: {i} adaptive nop, where a head suppresses its update by routing to a null token, and {ii} broadcast, where a sink aggregates and redistributes global information. In that case, sinks serve an analogous role: a safe destination when there is nothing useful to compute. Proposed interventions like gating or registers work because they implicitly target one or the other, revealing a duality between method and assumed mechanism: gating implicitly assumes nop; registers implicitly assume broadcast. Each mechanism leaves distinct traces (nop sinks exhibit negligible value norms; broadcast sinks induce low-rank outputs) which we formalize on synthetic tasks and use to derive practical diagnostics. Applied to pretrained vision transformers, these diagnostics reveal that both mechanisms exist at scale: sinks transition from CLS in early layers to patches in deeper layers, and concentrate in specialized heads. Strikingly, register tokens, designed for broadcast, are repurposed to also serve nop, confirming that neither intervention alone suffices. Combining gating with registers yields complementary gains in stability and performance. Overall, we find that the same attention pattern can reflect two very different computations and effective intervention requires first asking what the model is actually computing.

AI.tldr: 注意力沉溺（attention sink）现象在softmax transformer中普遍存在，但视觉上相似的沉溺模式可能反映两种根本不同的机制：自适应空操作（adaptive nop）和广播（broadcast）。本文通过合成任务形式化这两种机制的特征（nop沉溺的值范数极小，broadcast沉溺产生低秩输出），并提出实用诊断方法。在预训练视觉Transformer中，两种机制并存：沉溺从早期层的CLS令牌转移到深层块的补丁令牌，并集中在特定头中。研究发现寄存器令牌（为broadcast设计）也被用于nop，表明单一干预不足，结合门控与寄存器可获得互补增益。
AI.motivation: 注意力沉溺现象普遍存在但背后的计算机制未被充分理解，视觉上相同的沉溺模式可能对应完全不同的算法，而现有干预方法（如门控、寄存器）各自隐含地针对其中一种机制，导致干预效果不彻底。需要系统区分这两种机制并指导更有效的干预策略。
AI.method: 1) 在合成任务上形式化nop和broadcast两种机制的特征：nop沉溺的值范数极小，broadcast沉溺产生低秩输出；2) 基于这些特征提出实用诊断方法；3) 在预训练视觉Transformer上应用诊断方法分析沉溺分布；4) 测试门控和寄存器两种干预方法的互补性。
AI.result: 1) 两种机制在预训练视觉Transformer中并存：沉溺从早期层的CLS令牌向深层块的补丁令牌转移，并集中在特定注意力头中；2) 寄存器令牌（为broadcast设计）被模型实际重用于nop；3) 单独使用门控或寄存器均不足以完全解决问题，二者结合可带来稳定性和性能上的互补提升。
AI.conclusion: 相同的注意力模式可能反映两种完全不同的计算，有效的干预需要首先理解模型实际在计算什么。单一干预方法因隐含地针对特定机制而不充分，结合多种干预策略才能获得最佳效果。

title: REFLECT: Intervention-Supported Error Attribution for Silent Failures in LLM Agent Traces
summary: Large language model (LLM) agents now solve complex tasks through long plan-and-execution traces, yet the ability to locate errors in a completed traces still lags far behind, especially in the \emph{silent failure} regime. Existing approaches predict suspect steps via classifiers or LLM judges, or recover correct answers via retry, but none feed the intervention outcome back to \emph{refine the attribution itself}. We propose \methodname, a method that closes this gap by diagnosing a candidate error step, testing it through controlled replay with a diagnosis-specific patch, and using the verified outcome flip as contrastive evidence to refine the final attribution. Across four localization benchmarks spanning multi-hop reasoning across domains, \methodname achieves the highest localization accuracy among same-auditor methods across all four benchmarks, with the largest gains on structured tool-use traces, while providing actionable localization even when ground-truth answers are unavailable.

AI.tldr: 提出了一种通过诊断-测试-反馈闭环来定位LLM智能体轨迹中静默错误的方法，在四个基准测试上取得最优定位准确率。
AI.motivation: 现有方法要么通过分类器或LLM裁判预测可疑步骤，要么通过重试恢复正确答案，但都没有将干预结果反馈回来细化归因本身，导致在静默错误场景下定位能力不足。
AI.method: 诊断候选错误步骤，通过带有诊断特定补丁的受控重放进行测试，利用验证后的结果翻转作为对比证据来优化最终归因。
AI.result: 在四个涵盖多跳推理的定位基准上，该方法在所有同审核器方法中取得了最高的定位准确率，在结构化工具使用轨迹上提升最大，且即使在没有真实答案的情况下也能提供可操作的定位。
AI.conclusion: 通过引入"诊断-测试-反馈"闭环，将干预结果反馈用于细化归因，显著提升了LLM智能体轨迹中静默错误的定位能力。

title: Conditional Random Ordered Transport Spaces
summary: A small Wasserstein distance does not certify that a transformation is admissible. In evidence-constrained, semantic, causal, physical, monotone, or risk-sensitive learning, one must ask not only how far two probability laws are, but whether mass has moved in a direction allowed by available information. We introduce conditional random ordered transport spaces (CROTS), a class of \(L^0\)-valued spaces of random probability measures equipped with a Wasserstein ambient metric, a closed stochastic order, hard and soft ordered transport discrepancies, and a conditional risk functional for evaluating order violation under an evidence sigma-field. The central object is an order-admissible transport geometry for random measure-valued dynamics, distinct from cone-valued metrics, ordered Kantorovich constructions, random Wasserstein spaces alone, and model-specific residuals for generative paths. We develop the foundations of CROTS as a space theory for reliable distributional learning. The results include well-posedness and duality for hard and soft ordered transport, soft-to-hard variational convergence, measurability and completeness of the random lifted space, reductions to classical Wasserstein and ordered geometries, ordered geodesics, constrained barycenters and projections, conditional risk-transport duality, and separation of order-violating distributions. The main stability theorem shows that random learning dynamics may converge in the ambient Wasserstein metric while its local admissibility leakage follows a separate conditional order-risk recursion. The resulting asymptotic order-risk floor provides a mathematical language for evidence overreach, ordered distribution shift, robustness failure, and admissible distributional dynamics.

AI.tldr: 本文提出条件随机有序传输空间（CROTS），一类由随机概率测度构成的L^0值空间，配备Wasserstein环境度量、闭随机序、硬/软有序传输差异及条件风险泛函，用于刻画分布学习中质量传输的方向允许性，并建立其空间理论基础，包括良定性、对偶性、变分收敛、可测性、完备性、有序测地线、约束重心、条件风险-传输对偶及序违分布分离等结果。核心稳定性定理表明随机学习动力学可在环境Wasserstein度量下收敛，而局部允许性泄漏遵循独立的条件序-风险递归，最终序风险下限为证据过度延伸、有序分布偏移、鲁棒性失效及允许分布动力学提供了数学语言。
AI.motivation: 在证据约束、语义、因果、物理、单调或风险敏感学习中，仅衡量两个概率律之间的距离是不够的，还必须探明质量是否沿允许方向移动。小Wasserstein距离并不保证变换的可允许性，因此需要一种能同时考虑传输距离和方向允许性的理论框架。
AI.method: 引入CROTS空间，以随机概率测度为对象，融合Wasserstein环境度量、闭随机序、硬/软有序传输差异及条件风险泛函。通过随机提升空间的构造，建立有序允许传输几何，发展软到硬的变分收敛、可测性与完备性、有序测地线、约束重心与投影等理论工具。
AI.result: 建立了CROTS空间理论的完整基础：硬/软有序传输的良定性与对偶性、软到硬变分收敛、随机提升空间的可测性与完备性、经典Wasserstein与有序几何的约化关系、有序测地线、约束重心与投影、条件风险-传输对偶、序违分布分离。稳定性定理表明环境度量收敛与局部允许性泄漏遵循独立递归，得到渐近序风险下限。
AI.conclusion: CROTS为证据过度延伸、有序分布偏移、鲁棒性失效及可允许分布动力学提供了严格的数学语言，超越了传统锥值度量、有序Kantorovich构造、随机Wasserstein空间及生成路径的模型特定残差等方法。

title: DynaOD: Dynamic Origin-Destination Flow Generation with Discrete-to-Continuous Temporal Semantic Modeling
summary: Dynamic origin-destination (OD) flow generation seeks to synthesize realistic mobility dynamics from temporal context alone, without relying on historical OD observations. A key challenge is to translate semantic temporal signals into temporally coherent OD patterns while preserving the inherent spatial heterogeneity of urban regions. We propose DynaOD, a semantic-driven framework that models temporal dynamics through two complementary perspectives: discrete directional trends that characterize qualitative shifts in urban activity patterns, and continuous temporal evolution that captures how such shifts unfold over time. By jointly encoding these temporal semantics, the framework constructs time-varying region representations that condition pretrained static OD generators in a lightweight and plug-and-play fashion. This modular design further supports scalable deployment and cross-city transferability. Extensive experiments on large-scale real-world datasets show that our method consistently outperforms representative baselines in both predictive accuracy and distributional fidelity. Code is publicly available at https://github.com/csjiezhao/DynaOD.

AI.tldr: 本文提出DynaOD框架，通过联合编码离散方向趋势和连续时间演化两种时间语义，以轻量级即插即用的方式调节预训练的静态OD生成器，实现无需历史OD观测的语义驱动的动态OD流生成，在准确性和分布 fidelity 上均优于基线方法。
AI.motivation: 动态OD流生成的关键挑战在于如何将语义化的时间信号转化为时序连贯的OD模式，同时保留城市区域固有的空间异质性。现有方法缺乏对时间语义的有效建模。
AI.method: 提出DynaOD框架，从两个互补视角建模时间动态：离散方向趋势（定性刻画城市活动模式的变化）和连续时间演化（捕捉变化随时间展开的过程），通过联合编码构建时变区域表示，以轻量级即插即用方式调节预训练静态OD生成器。
AI.result: 在大规模真实数据集上的实验表明，该方法在预测准确性和分布保真度方面均优于代表性基线方法，并支持可扩展部署和跨城市迁移。
AI.conclusion: DynaOD通过语义驱动的时间建模有效解决了动态OD流生成问题，其模块化设计具有良好的可扩展性和跨城市迁移能力。

title: TRUST-SCF: Transformer-based Risk Understanding and Scoring for Transactional Supply Chain Finance
summary: Supply Chain Finance (SCF) and LendTech platforms need credit scoring systems that respond to evolving transaction behavior, repayment delays, and active exposure. We propose TRUST-SCF, a transformer-based framework for transaction-level risk prediction and dynamic credit scoring. Each user history is represented as a sequence of transaction tokens containing utilization, repayment delay and transaction position. The main contributions are: (1) a financially aligned attention bias that combines utilization similarity and recency, enabling the model to compare repayment behavior under comparable exposure conditions; (2) continuous repayment-delay prediction in a log-transformed target space, reducing the influence of extreme delays while improving sensitivity to short-delay behavior and (3) a label-efficient credit-scoring pipeline in which the final credit score is not trained using any explicit external credit-score label, but is instead derived from predicted delay, potential risk over simulated utilization, actual unpaid exposure, and nonlinear calibration. Experiments on real transaction data from more than 300,000 transactions show that TRUST-SCF improves delay prediction over sequential baselines and produces scores that are strongly associated with future repayment behavior. These results suggest that TRUST-SCF is a practical framework for adaptive credit scoring and transaction-level risk mitigation in SCF and LendTech environments.

AI.tldr: TRUST-SCF是一个基于Transformer的框架，利用交易级序列数据（包含利用率、还款延迟和交易位置）进行动态信用评分，通过财务对齐的注意力偏置、对数变换的连续延迟预测和无外部标签的信用评分流程，在30万+真实交易数据上优于序列基线模型。
AI.motivation: 供应链金融和LendTech平台需要能够响应交易行为演变、还款延迟和活跃风险敞口的信用评分系统，现有方法难以捕捉交易序列中的动态风险模式。
AI.method: (1) 将用户历史建模为交易令牌序列（利用率、还款延迟、交易位置）；(2) 提出财务对齐的注意力偏置（融合利用率相似性和时间近因性）；(3) 对数变换空间中的连续还款延迟预测，减少极端延迟影响；(4) 无需外部信用评分标签，从预测延迟、模拟利用率下的潜在风险、实际未偿风险敞口和非线性校准中推导最终信用评分。
AI.result: 在30万+真实交易数据上，TRUST-SCF在延迟预测任务上优于序列基线模型，生成的信用评分与未来还款行为强相关。
AI.conclusion: TRUST-SCF是一种适用于SCF和LendTech环境的自适应信用评分和交易级风险缓解的实用框架。

title: Graph2Idea:Retrieval-Augmented Scientific Idea Generation with Graph-Structured Contexts
summary: Generating novel, feasible, and high-quality research ideas is an important yet challenging task in scientific discovery.Recent Large Language Model (LLM)-based methods often ground idea generation with retrieved literature, but the retrieved evidence is usually provided as flat text, such as titles, abstracts, or summaries. Such flat contexts may contain redundant or weakly relevant information, while making cross-paper relations among problems, methods, mechanisms, and findings difficult to identify and trace.To address this challenge, we propose Graph2Idea, a knowledge graph-guided framework for retrieval-augmented scientific idea generation.Graph2Idea first retrieves papers according to the input topic, transforms them into structured knowledge triples, and dynamically constructs a target-centered knowledge graph to make literature relations explicit.It then extracts compact graph-derived contexts that retain target-relevant relational evidence while reducing noisy textual input.Based on these contexts, a two-stage generation process first identifies promising research directions and then guides the LLM to synthesize candidate ideas from graph-grounded evidence.Experiments on a scientific idea generation benchmark show that Graph2Idea outperforms representative baselines under the automatic evaluation protocol.Compared with the strongest baseline scores, it improves Novelty from 0.45 to 0.52, Quality from 0.24 to 0.29, and Feasibility from 0.22 to 0.28.These results suggest that graph-structured evidence helps LLMs generate research ideas through more explicit, compact, and traceable recombination of prior scientific knowledge.

AI.tldr: 本文提出Graph2Idea框架，通过知识图谱引导的检索增强生成方法实现科学想法生成。该框架将检索到的论文转化为结构化知识三元组，构建以目标为中心的知识图谱，提取紧凑的图上下文，并采用两阶段生成流程（先识别研究方向，再生成候选想法）。实验表明，在Novelty、Quality、Feasibility指标上分别提升至0.52、0.29、0.28，优于所有基线。
AI.motivation: 现有基于LLM的科学想法生成方法通常使用检索到的文献作为扁平文本（如标题、摘要）进行输入，但这类上下文包含冗余或弱相关信息，且难以识别和追溯问题、方法、机制、发现之间的跨论文关系。
AI.method: Graph2Idea框架：1) 根据输入主题检索论文；2) 将论文转化为结构化知识三元组，动态构建以目标为中心的知识图谱；3) 提取紧凑的图派生上下文，保留目标相关的关系证据并减少噪声；4) 两阶段生成：先识别有前景的研究方向，再基于图证据引导LLM合成候选想法。
AI.result: 在科学想法生成基准上，Graph2Idea优于所有基线。与最强基线相比，Novelty从0.45提升至0.52，Quality从0.24提升至0.29，Feasibility从0.22提升至0.28。
AI.conclusion: 图结构化的证据能够帮助LLM通过更明确、更紧凑、更可追溯的先前科学知识重组来生成研究想法，证明了知识图谱在检索增强科学想法生成中的有效性。

title: LogNEO: A GPT-Neo Reinforcement Learning Framework for Accurate Real-Time Log Anomaly Detection
summary: Detecting anomalies in large-scale system logs is critical for the reliability and security of modern computing infrastructure. We present LogNEO, a log anomaly detector built on EleutherAI's GPT-Neo (1.3B parameters) and fine-tuned with a novel partial-credit, exponentially decaying position-aware reward scheme combined with cross-entropy regularisation via Proximal Policy Optimisation (PPO). The position-aware reward explicitly models prediction difficulty: early positions receive higher rewards for correct predictions, while later positions incur stronger penalties for errors. LogNEO attains F1-scores of 0.927, 0.913, and 0.984 on the HDFS, BGL, and Thunderbird benchmarks, improving recall by up to 6 percentage points over the prior state-of-the-art LogGPT while maintaining comparable precision. A production microservice deployment over Apache Kafka, Redis, and TensorRT-accelerated inference demonstrates 45 ms end-to-end latency at 15,000 events per second.

AI.tldr: LogNEO是基于GPT-Neo (1.3B)微调的大规模日志异常检测系统，采用偏置指数衰减位置感知奖励与PPO优化，在HDFS/BGL/Thunderbird上F1达0.927/0.913/0.984，召回率相比LogGPT提升6个百分点，生产线部署延迟45ms@15K events/s。
AI.motivation: 大规模系统日志异常检测对计算基础设施的可靠性和安全性至关重要，现有方法在检测精度和延迟方面仍有改进空间。
AI.method: 基于EleutherAI GPT-Neo（1.3B参数）微调，提出偏置指数衰减位置感知奖励机制（早期位置正确预测高奖励，后期位置错误强惩罚），结合交叉熵正则化的近端策略优化（PPO）进行训练。
AI.result: HDFS、BGL、Thunderbird数据集上F1-score分别为0.927、0.913、0.984；召回率较此前最先进的LogGPT提升最多6个百分点，精度持平；生产线部署端到端延迟45ms，吞吐量15,000 events/s。
AI.conclusion: LogNEO通过位置感知奖励设计的PPO微调策略有效提升了日志异常检测的召回率，同时保持高精度和低延迟，验证了在大规模生产环境中的实用性。

title: ComplexConstraints and Beyond: Expert Rubrics for RLVR
comment: Accepted to the GEM workshop at ACL 2026: https://gem-workshop.com/
summary: As LLM capabilities advance rapidly, the evaluation methods used to assess them increasingly lag behind. Traditional benchmarks relied on programmatic verification of narrow, surface-level constraints, but real-world instruction following and agentic tasks demand assessment of nuanced, context-dependent behaviors that resist simple scripted checks. We present a systematic analysis of expert-curated rubric-based evaluation as an alternative paradigm, drawing on empirical evidence from two domains: complex instruction following and enterprise agentic tasks. We first articulate five design principles for constructing high-quality rubrics, including Maximum Viable Atomicity, intent-aware criterion design, and iterative LLM-judge calibration. To validate these principles, we introduce ComplexConstraints, a new expert-curated instruction-following dataset in which each prompt is paired with 10-40 atomic rubric criteria. We demonstrate that these expert rubrics are not only better evaluation instruments but also highly effective training signals: training on approximately 1,000 ComplexConstraints examples yields +15.5% improvement for a 4B-parameter model and +12.2% for a 235B-parameter model on instruction following, while single-epoch RL training on a rubric-graded enterprise environment produces gains that transfer to out-of-distribution benchmarks the model was never trained on (+4.5% BFCL, +7.4% Tau2-Bench, +6.8% Tool-Decathlon). Our findings establish that expert-authored rubrics improve both the measurement and the development of frontier LLM capabilities, serving as effective evaluation and RL training signals.

AI.tldr: 本文系统分析了专家编写的基于评分标准（rubric）的评估范式，提出了高质量评分标准设计的五条原则（最大可行原子性、意图导向的标准设计、迭代LLM裁判校准等），并通过两个领域（复杂指令遵循和企业智能体任务）的实证研究证明：专家编写的评分标准不仅是更好的评估工具，还能作为有效的训练信号——在指令遵循任务上提升4B模型15.5%、235B模型12.2%，在智能体任务上通过单轮RL训练实现跨分布迁移（BFCL +4.5%, Tau2-Bench +7.4%, Tool-Decathlon +6.8%）。
AI.motivation: 传统基准测试依赖程序化验证，仅能评估狭窄、表面的约束，无法应对真实世界中需要细粒度、上下文敏感行为评估的指令遵循和智能体任务。现有评估方法已明显落后于LLM能力的快速发展，亟需更有效的评估范式。
AI.method: (1) 提出评分标准设计的五条原则；(2) 构建ComplexConstraints数据集（每个提示配10-40个原子评分标准）；(3) 在两个领域（复杂指令遵循和企业智能体任务）进行实验验证；(4) 分别使用监督训练和单轮RL训练评估评分标准作为训练信号的效果。
AI.result: 指令遵循：训练约1000个ComplexConstraints样本，4B模型提升15.5%，235B模型提升12.2%。智能体任务：单轮RL训练后，跨分布迁移至BFCL (+4.5%)、Tau2-Bench (+7.4%)、Tool-Decathlon (+6.8%)。
AI.conclusion: 专家编写的评分标准既改进了前沿LLM能力的测量（作为更优的评估工具），也促进了能力的发展（作为有效的RL训练信号），是评估与训练一体化的有效范式。

title: A Regret Minimization Framework on Preference Learning in Large Language Models
summary: Reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards (RLVR) has enabled progress on reasoning-intensive tasks by relying on task-specific verifiers that provide automated correctness signals. However, many realistic language tasks are difficult to equip with reliable verifiers, motivating a growing reliance on reinforcement learning from human feedback (RLHF). In this setting, we argue that a closer examination of how human feedback should be interpreted is essential. We introduce Regret-based Preference Optimization $(\textbf{RePO})$, which reframes RLHF through $\textit{regret minimization}$ rather than reward maximization. Human preferences are often shaped by $\textit{prospective}$ anticipation of outcomes and $\textit{counterfactual}$ comparisons to alternative behaviors, rather than by immediate, outcome-independent utility. $\textbf{RePO}$ captures this structure by modeling preferences as behavior-conditioned assessments of relative suboptimality. Experiments on mathematical reasoning benchmarks and human preference datasets demonstrate consistent performance gains, indicating that $\textbf{RePO}$ is an effective and human-aligned approach for training large language models.

AI.tldr: 本文提出基于遗憾的偏好优化（RePO），将RLHF重新框架为遗憾最小化而非奖励最大化，通过建模人类偏好中前瞻性预期与反事实比较的结构，在数学推理和人类偏好数据集上取得持续性能提升。
AI.motivation: 许多真实语言任务难以配备可靠的验证器，而现有RLHF方法未充分考虑人类偏好的内在结构——人类偏好往往由前瞻性结果预期和反事实比较塑造，而非即时的、与结果无关的效用。
AI.method: 提出Regret-based Preference Optimization (RePO)，将RLHF重新框架为遗憾最小化问题，把人类偏好建模为行为条件下的相对次优性评估（behavior-conditioned assessments of relative suboptimality），捕捉偏好中的前瞻性和反事实结构。
AI.result: 在数学推理基准和人类偏好数据集上均取得一致性的性能提升。
AI.conclusion: RePO是一种有效且更符合人类对齐的方法，可用于训练大型语言模型。

title: Late-Layer Fusion is Enough: Dual-Path Vision Token Routing for Multimodal Large Language Models under Visual Saturation
comment: 18 pages, 4 figures. Submitted to Pattern Recognition
summary: Multimodal large language models (MLLMs) commonly inherit the deep, symmetric Transformer backbone designed for unimodal text modeling, and apply the same computation uniformly to image and language tokens. This design overlooks a key modality asymmetry: image and text tokens differ substantially in information density, redundancy, and required reasoning depth. Through a layer-wise analysis of LLaVA-1.5, we observe that vision tokens tend to saturate in the middle layers. Specifically, text-to-image attention decreases from 0.68 at layer 0 to 0.07 by layer 4, and stabilizes near 0.04 after layer 18, whereas text tokens continue to benefit from deep semantic processing. These findings suggest a mismatch between architectural symmetry and depth-asynchronous modality evolution, resulting in redundant visual computation and possible drift in perceptual representations during deep task-specific adaptation. Motivated by this, we propose Dual-Path Vision Token Routing (DPVR), a modality-asymmetric routing framework for efficient MLLMs. Its core instantiation, DPVR-LF (Late-Layer Fusion), routes vision tokens at the saturation point into a one-layer trainable side branch, runs a thirteen-layer text-only forward that skips image positions in the deep stack, and re-fuses the visual and textual streams only at the final layer. With approximately 3% trainable parameters, DPVR-LF preserves competitive multimodal performance on standard benchmarks while reducing visual computation in the deep Transformer stack. The results challenge the conventional assumption that vision tokens must traverse all deep language-model layers, and indicate that a single late fusion layer can be sufficient for maintaining strong perceptual competence in LLaVA-style MLLMs.

AI.tldr: 本文提出DPVR-LF，一种非对称模态路由框架，通过在视觉token饱和点将其路由至浅层旁路分支，仅用约3%可训练参数并在最后层融合视觉与文本流，在保持多模态性能的同时显著减少深度Transformer中的视觉计算量。
AI.motivation: 现有MLLMs继承统一深度Transformer架构，对图像和文本token施加相同计算，忽略了二者在信息密度、冗余度和推理深度上的模态不对称性，导致视觉计算冗余和感知表征漂移。
AI.method: 提出DPVR（Dual-Path Vision Token Routing），其核心实例DPVR-LF在视觉token饱和点将其路由至单层可训练侧分支，文本单独通过13层深层Transformer，仅在最终层重新融合视觉和文本流。
AI.result: 仅需约3%可训练参数，DPVR-LF在标准基准上保持竞争力的多模态性能，同时大幅减少深层堆叠中的视觉计算量。
AI.conclusion: 挑战了视觉token必须遍历所有深层语言模型层的传统假设，表明单层后期融合足以维持LLaVA风格MLLMs的强感知能力。

title: Explaining Data Mixing Scaling Laws
summary: Recent research has established empirical scaling laws to predict model performance on multi-domain data mixtures. However, a theoretical understanding of these model loss behaviors remains absent. In this work, we propose a unified framework to explain the underlying mechanics of data mixing. Our approach extends theoretical perspectives originally developed for standard neural scaling laws (e.g., Kaplan and Chinchilla) to the multi-domain setting. Based on the distributional assumption that domains overlap on fundamental skills while diverging on specialized skills, we identify two key factors that govern the domain losses of models trained on different data mixtures: \textit{Capacity Competition}, where the allocation of finite model capacity couples domain losses globally, and \textit{Noise Reduction}, where optimal weights shift toward harder-to-learn domains to minimize overall noise. Empirical evaluations show that our framework outperforms existing baselines by fitting the loss landscape with a lower Mean Relative Error and identifying higher-performing training mixtures. Most importantly, our model successfully extrapolates across scales, predicting highly effective mixtures for large, unseen scales using parameters fitted on smaller ones. In addition, our model achieves these results using significantly fewer parameters compared to previous empirical laws. Our code is available at https://github.com/meiqwq/Explaining-Data-Mixing-Scaling-Laws.

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一个统一的理论框架来解释多领域数据混合的缩放定律，基于领域间共享基础技能、差异专业技能的分布假设，识别出容量竞争和噪声降低两个关键因素，在更少参数下实现了比现有经验定律更优的拟合和跨尺度外推能力。
AI.motivation: 现有研究已建立多领域数据混合的经验缩放定律来预测模型性能，但缺乏对这些损失行为的理论理解。本文旨在填补这一理论空白。
AI.method: 将标准神经缩放定律（如Kaplan和Chinchilla）的理论视角扩展到多领域场景。假设不同领域在基础技能上有重叠、在专业技能上存在差异，识别出容量竞争（有限模型容量在不同领域间的分配导致损失耦合）和噪声降低（最优权重倾向于让模型更关注难以学习的领域以最小化整体噪声）两个关键因素。
AI.result: 在损失曲面拟合上取得了更低的平均相对误差，能够识别出更高性能的训练混合比例，并且在更少参数下实现了跨尺度的有效外推（用小尺度拟合的参数预测大尺度上的最优混合）。
AI.conclusion: 该理论框架不仅解释了多领域数据混合中的缩放行为，还以更简洁的参数化方式实现了比经验定律更好的预测性能，为数据混合策略提供了理论指导。

title: Vision Language Model Helps Private Information De-Identification in Vision Data
summary: Visual Language Models (VLMs) have gained significant popularity due to their remarkable ability. While various methods exist to enhance privacy in text-based applications, privacy risks associated with visual inputs remain largely overlooked such as Protected Health Information (PHI) in medical images. To tackle this problem, two key tasks: accurately localizing sensitive text and processing it to ensure privacy protection should be performed. To address this issue, we introduce VisShield (Vision Privacy Shield), an end-to-end framework designed to enhance the privacy awareness of VLMs. Our framework consists of two key components: a specialized instruction-tuning dataset OPTIC (Optical Privacy Text Instruction Collection) and a tailored training methodology. The dataset provides diverse privacy-oriented prompts that guide VLMs to perform targeted Optical Character Recognition (OCR) for precise localization of sensitive text, while the training strategy ensures effective adaptation of VLMs to privacy-preserving tasks. Specifically, our approach ensures that VLMs recognize privacy-sensitive text and output precise bounding boxes for detected entities, allowing for effective masking of sensitive information. Extensive experiments demonstrate that our framework significantly outperforms existing approaches in handling private information, paving the way for privacy-preserving applications in vision-language models. Our dataset and code can be found here.

AI.tldr: VisShield是一个端到端框架，通过专用数据集OPTIC和定制训练策略，增强视觉语言模型对医学图像等视觉输入中敏感文本（如PHI）的隐私保护能力，实现精准定位与掩码处理。
AI.motivation: 现有隐私保护方法主要针对文本应用，而视觉输入（如医学图像中的受保护健康信息）的隐私风险被严重忽视，亟需解决方案。
AI.method: 提出VisShield框架，包含两个核心组件：(1) OPTIC数据集——提供多样化隐私导向提示，引导VLM执行定向OCR以精确定位敏感文本；(2) 定制训练策略——使VLM适应隐私保护任务，识别隐私敏感文本并输出精确边界框，从而有效掩码敏感信息。
AI.result: 大量实验表明，VisShield在处理隐私信息方面显著优于现有方法。
AI.conclusion: VisShield为视觉语言模型中的隐私保护应用铺平了道路，有效解决了视觉输入中的隐私风险问题。

title: Frequency-Domain Latent Attention Gating for Cross-Domain Token Aggregation
summary: Token aggregation is a common bottleneck in models that map token representations to sample-level predictions, yet most pooling methods operate only in the original token domain. We propose FLaG, a plug-in aggregation module that transforms token representations with the real FFT, summarizes spectral components with learnable latent queries, applies a channel-wise gate, and reconstructs enhanced time-domain tokens for final pooling. We evaluate FLaG on antimicrobial peptide (AMP) activity prediction with ESM2, image classification with ResNet18 on CIFAR-10 and CIFAR-100, and text classification with RoBERTa on IMDB and GLUE. FLaG achieves its clearest gains on the ESM2-8M antimicrobial peptide tasks and on CIFAR-100, while remaining competitive with strong text baselines on IMDB and GLUE. Then we probe its behavior on the AMP setting with band knockouts, gate summaries, residue perturbations, latent-query readouts, and structure-proxy stratification. We find that low-frequency bands contribute the most overall, and the remaining higher-band pattern is more sample-specific. The gate acts as a broadly shared spectral reweighting stage and the cross-attention patterns are sample-specific with mild query-wise differentiation, and higher-helix peptides exhibit stronger average spectral sensitivity in both bacteria. The supplementary materials, source code and data are released at https://www.healthinformaticslab.org/supp/ and https://github.com/Kewei2023/AMPCliff/tree/FLaG.

AI.tldr: FLaG是一种基于FFT的即插即用聚合模块，通过频域变换、可学习潜查询和通道门控提升token聚合能力，在AMP活性预测、图像分类和文本分类任务上表现优异，并深入分析了频带贡献、门控行为和跨注意力模式。
AI.motivation: 现有池化方法仅在原始token域进行操作，成为模型从token表示到样本级预测的瓶颈，缺乏在频域中进行token聚合的有效手段。
AI.method: 提出FLaG模块：先对token表示应用实FFT变换到频域，用可学习的潜查询汇总频谱成分，经过通道级门控后，重建增强的时域token用于最终池化。
AI.result: 在ESM2-8M抗菌肽任务和CIFAR-100上获得最显著提升，在IMDB和GLUE文本任务上与强基线持平；通过频带消融、门控分析、残基扰动等探针实验揭示了低频贡献最大、门控为共享重加权、交叉注意力样本特异等发现。
AI.conclusion: FLaG作为一种即插即用的频域聚合方法，能有效提升多种任务的性能，并提供了对模型行为（频带贡献、门控机制、注意力模式等）的可解释性分析。

title: Reliable to Expressive: A Curriculum for Rubric-Following Safety Judges
comment: Accepted to ICML 2026 Workshop on AIWILDS
summary: Safety judges are increasingly deployed to evaluate model outputs against evolving criteria, yet recent meta-evaluation work shows they remain brittle under prompt and rubric variation, with false negative-rate swings of up to 0.24 reported for stylistic perturbations alone. We argue that safety judgment is fundamentally a rubric-following problem: a robust judge must apply the given evaluation criteria consistently across rubric formulations rather than memorize one specific template. We propose a training strategy that combines (i) instance-conditioned dynamic rubrics generated from prompt-response-label triples to expose the judge to the variability of evaluation criteria, and (ii) a reliable-to-expressive curriculum that begins with clean fixed-rubric supervision and progressively introduces noisier dynamic-rubric data. We evaluate on a single human-labeled set under three contrasting rubric prompts (HarmBench-style, ShieldGemma-style, and a domain-specific rubric). Our 12B curriculum judge achieves 94.12-94.88% accuracy across the three rubrics with a cross-rubric range of only 0.76, outperforming general-purpose LLMs, dedicated safety classifiers, and reasoning-oriented judges up to 30B in both peak accuracy and stability. An ablation shows that naively mixing dynamic rubrics into SFT increases cross rubric variance (1.44 -> 3.60); only the curriculum schedule recovers and improves on the fixed rubric baseline (variance 0.76).

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种结合实例条件动态rubric和可靠到表达性课程训练的策略，用于提升安全判断器在不同rubric下的鲁棒性。12B课程判断器在三种对比rubric上达到94.12-94.88%准确率，跨rubric范围仅0.76，优于通用LLM和专用安全分类器。消融实验表明，简单混合动态rubric会增大跨rubric方差，而课程调度能恢复并改进基线。
AI.motivation: 现有安全判断器在提示和rubric变化下表现脆弱，风格扰动即可导致0.24的假阴性率波动。作者认为安全判断本质上是遵循rubric的问题，需要让判断器学会一致地应用不同表述的评价标准，而非记忆特定模板。
AI.method: 提出双管齐下的训练策略：(i) 从提示-响应-标签三元组生成实例条件动态rubric，暴露判断器于评价标准的变异性；(ii) 采用可靠到表达性课程，从干净固定rubric监督开始，逐步引入噪声更大的动态rubric数据。
AI.result: 12B课程判断器在HarmBench、ShieldGemma和领域特定三种rubric下达到94.12-94.88%准确率，跨rubric范围仅0.76。消融实验显示，在SFT中简单混合动态rubric使跨rubric方差从1.44增至3.60，而课程调度将方差降至0.76。
AI.conclusion: 课程式动态rubric训练能显著提升安全判断器在不同评估标准下的准确性和稳定性，优于通用LLM和专用安全分类器。

title: Neural Field Tokenizations with Hierarchy and Spatial Locality Priors
summary: Neural fields parameterize data as functions from coordinates to values, providing a unified framework for representation learning across modalities. Existing approaches are dominated by per-sample meta-learning, which scales poorly due to memory-intensive inner-loop optimization. The natural alternative -- feed-forward encoding -- typically introduces modality-specific assumptions, sacrificing the generality that makes learning with neural fields attractive. We argue that locality and hierarchy are useful priors for learning field representations that can be injected without compromising modality-agnosticism. We propose LH-NeF, a framework to learn general-purpose tokenized representations of continuous signals. A locality-preserving hierarchical encoder maps raw coordinate-value field observations to structured tokens, from which the field is reconstructed during training. By replacing meta-learning's inner loop with a single forward pass, LH-NeF uses 42$\times$ less memory and supports 133$\times$ larger batches than the strongest modality-agnostic baseline. Across images, 3D shapes, and climate fields, our learned representations match or exceed performance of modality-agnostic, modality-specific, and specialized generative neural field baselines on both reconstruction and downstream tasks.

AI.tldr: 提出LH-NeF框架，利用局部性和层次性先验，通过前馈编码替代元学习，实现模态无关的神经场表示学习，内存减少42倍，批大小提升133倍，在图像、3D形状和气候场任务中达到或超越现有基线。
AI.motivation: 现有神经场方法以逐样本元学习为主，内循环优化内存开销大、扩展性差；前馈编码则引入模态特定假设，牺牲了神经场的通用性。作者希望在不牺牲模态无关性的前提下，利用局部性和层次性先验来提升神经场表示学习的效率和性能。
AI.method: 提出LH-NeF框架，包含一个保持局部性的层次化编码器，将原始坐标-值场观测映射为结构化token，训练时从这些token重建场。用单次前向传播替代元学习的内循环优化。
AI.result: 相比最强模态无关基线，内存减少42倍，批大小提升133倍。在图像、3D形状和气候场任务上，重建和下游任务性能匹配或超越模态无关、模态特定及专用生成神经场基线。
AI.conclusion: 局部性和层次性是有效的模态无关先验，前馈编码范式能够在保持通用性的同时大幅提升神经场的学习效率和可扩展性。

title: IMUG-Bench: Benchmarking Unified Multimodal Models on Interleaved Understanding and Generation
summary: In recent years, unified multimodal models (UMMs) have emerged to support both understanding and generation within a single framework. Mastering dynamic, multi-turn interleaved image-text dialogues is a crucial task for UMMs in real-world applications. However, existing benchmarks fail to evaluate this important task, as they are often limited to single-turn or static settings, and typically overlook exposure bias in multi-turn interactions. To bridge this gap, we propose IMUG-Bench, a comprehensive benchmark for multi-turn interleaved image-text dialogue of UMMs that jointly evaluates their understanding and generation capabilities. Our IMUG-Bench comprises three classes: Static Spatial, Temporal Causal, and Hybrid, covering 3,113 samples and 12,034 interaction turns. It also includes dynamic understanding questions, thereby supporting evaluation that better reflects real-world multi-turn interaction scenarios. Large-scale experiments on IMUG-Bench systematically evaluate mainstream open-source and closed-source UMMs, revealing their capability boundaries and failure modes, and uncovering pronounced exposure bias on the generation side in multi-turn interactions. We further explore several test-time scaling strategies, including Chain-of-Thought, Self-Verification, and Best-of-N Sampling, which effectively improve generation accuracy and mitigate exposure bias in generation tasks. These findings provide insights into enhancing the robustness and multi-turn interaction capability of future UMMs.

AI.tldr: 提出IMUG-Bench基准，用于评估统一多模态模型在多轮交错图文对话中的理解与生成能力，发现并缓解了生成侧的曝光偏差问题。
AI.motivation: 现有基准无法评估统一多模态模型在多轮交错图文对话中的表现，仅局限于单轮或静态设置，且忽略了多轮交互中的曝光偏差。
AI.method: 构建包含静态空间、时间因果和混合三类共3,113个样本、12,034个交互轮的IMUG-Bench基准，设计动态理解问题以模拟真实多轮交互场景，并探索测试时扩展策略（思维链、自我验证、Best-of-N采样）。
AI.result: 系统评估了主流开源和闭源UMMs，揭示了能力边界和失败模式，发现多轮交互中生成侧存在显著的曝光偏差，且探索的测试时扩展策略能有效提升生成准确率并缓解该偏差。
AI.conclusion: IMUG-Bench为评估和提升UMMs在多轮交互中的鲁棒性提供了重要基准和见解。

title: Public Machine Learning Solver Framework for Novices in the Machine Learning Domain
summary: Solving machine learning problems is complex and typically reserved for experts. Over the past two decades, systems have emerged to support non-experts. Based on our review, we identify three categories: (1) fully automated AutoML systems, (2) expert cheat sheets for algorithm selection, and (3) decision-support systems using selection criteria (accuracy, transparency, data requirements). We propose a new platform combining categories 2 and 3 to deliver semi-automated, intelligent solution recommendations for non-experts. Unlike existing approaches that recommend a single algorithm, our platform suggests a complete pipeline tailored to the user's problem. It integrates expert-defined selection criteria with transfer learning and automatically extracts data characteristics (e.g., class imbalance, missing values) from user-provided datasets. The platform uses first-order logic to reason over its knowledge base and recommends suitable algorithms ranked by relevance. It features a user-friendly interface and connects to a crowdsourcing platform for ML experts, ensuring continuous updates. The platform is built incrementally, allowing seamless integration of new algorithms, criteria, and domain knowledge. To our knowledge, this is the first free, publicly accessible online framework that systematically captures and operationalizes expert knowledge to guide non-experts in solving ML problems in a structured, transparent manner.

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一个面向非专家的半自动化机器学习平台，结合专家规则与迁移学习，能够根据用户数据集自动提取特征并推荐完整的机器学习流水线，而非单一算法。该平台基于一阶逻辑推理，支持众包更新，是首个免费、公开且系统化运用专家知识指导非专家解决ML问题的在线框架。
